name: Franky Barragan
title: Technical Program Management • Community Leadership • Systems Architecture
bio: I manage complex enterprise infrastructure, lead community programs, and guide teams through rapid technical shifts[cite: 2]. My strength is working as a translator between engineering teams and non-technical stakeholders so everyone stays aligned during major migrations and rollouts[cite: 2].

certifications.name: VMware Certified Professional – Data Center Virtualization (VCP-DCV)

story_title: Bridging Deep Technical Work and Community Impact
story_text: My career has run on two parallel tracks: hands-on infrastructure engineering and large-scale community leadership[cite: 2]. On the technical side, I ran a 1,000+ VM environment, where I cut recurring critical outages down to a rare event through direct root cause analysis[cite: 2]. On the community side, I have led global tech user groups for tens of thousands of members, mentored volunteer leaders, and kept member trust intact through massive organizational transitions[cite: 2]. I find that the best community programs are built with the same discipline as reliable infrastructure: look for the actual pain points, build for resilience, and track metrics that truly matter[cite: 2].
anecdote_title: Turning a Weekly Fire Drill Into a Non-Event
anecdote_text: I was the only VMware engineer responsible for infrastructure supporting over 1,000 virtual machines, and critical incidents were hitting us almost every week[cite: 2]. Rather than just firefighting, I dug into the core patterns of why our systems kept breaking, hardened the layout against those exact failure modes, and put proactive tracking in place[cite: 2]. Within a few years, critical incidents dropped from weekly to less than one a year and stayed there[cite: 2].

soft_skills.anecdote: Managed critical infrastructure stability without a dedicated safety net, stepping in as the sole escalation point for a 1,000+ VM footprint[cite: 2].
Launched a VMware Cloud Foundation exam prep series that brought in over 1,000 signups within 48 hours, adapting the topics in real time based on community tracking data[cite: 2].
Mentored over 60 volunteer VMUG leaders across the West Coast, building reusable playbooks to help new teams get up and running faster[cite: 2].
